methods.name: wbportfolio.models.transactions.trades.Trade.subquery_shares_per_underlying_instrument
documentation:
Returns a Subquery that returns the shares at a certain price date
or 0

Arguments:
    val_date {datetime.date} -- The  date that is used to determine which tradesare filtered

Keyword Arguments:
    underlying_instrument_name {str} -- The reference to the underlying_instrument pk of the outer query (default: {"pk"})

Returns:
    django.db.models.Subquery -- Subquery containing the sum of shares of each underlying_instrument

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.transactions.trades.Trade.subquery_net_money
documentation:
Return a subquery which computes the net negative/positive money per underlying_instrument

Arguments:
    val_date1 {datetime.date} -- The start date, including
    val_date2 {datetime.date} -- The end date, including

Keyword Arguments:
    underlying_instrument_name {str} -- The reference to the underlying_instrument pk from the outer query (default: {"pk"})

Returns:
    django.db.models.Subquery -- The subquery containing the net negative money per underlying_instrument

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.transactions.trade_proposals.TradeProposal.create_or_update_trades
documentation:
This function talk to the trading service layer in order to generate a list of valid trades to attach to the proposal

Args:
    target_portfolio: The target portfolio that the trades needs to execute to. Absence of position means a sell
    effective_portfolio: The current or effective portfolio to derivative effective weight from. Absence of position means a buy
    reset: If true, delete the current attached trades

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.transactions.claim.Claim.subquery_assets_under_management_for_account
documentation:
Returns a subquery which annotates the assets in USD for each Sub Account based on a given queryset

Arguments:
    claims {QuerySet<commission.Claim>} -- Prefiltered queryset of claims
    price_date {datetime.date} -- the date which is used to calculate everything
    account_key {str} -- the outer reference to the sub account pk (default: account__pk)

Returns:
    Subquery -- Sub Account with assets in USD
A customer can claim that a trade or part of a trade was executed my them

inner_classes.name: wbportfolio.models.asset.AssetPosition.PriceDenotation
documentation: VALUATION                       #

The Asset Model holds all information needed to compute the value of the asset and contribution to its wbportfolio. All
information which are regarded as Meta-Information, such as country, industry, currency allocation is held in the
asset information, which is accessible through a FK, depending on the asset type of the asset.

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.portfolio_synchronization.PortfolioSynchronization.synchronize
documentation:
This function compute the new portfolio composition after synchronization (returns from `_import_method`) for a
given date and either update or create the portfolio or create a trade proposal given the new portfolio constituent.

```
param portfolio

The portfolio to synchronize the positions from

param sync_date

The date at which we need to synchronize the given portfolio

param task_execution_datetime

An optional datetime specifying at which time this task was initially executed.

param override_execution_datetime_validity

If true, we don't valide 

param kwargs

keyword arguments
```

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.portfolio_synchronization.PortfolioSynchronization.synchronize_as_task_si
documentation: Utility function that returns the signature of the synchronize method

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.portfolio_synchronization.PortfolioSynchronization._tasks_signature
documentation:
Gather all tasks that needs to run under this synchronization job as a list of celery signatures.
This method is expected to be implemented at each inheriting class.
\:param args: list
\:param kwargs: dict
\:return: list[signature]

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.portfolio_synchronization.PortfolioSynchronization._default_callback
documentation:
Recursively calculates the position for a portfolio

Arguments:
    portfolio {portfolio.Portfolio} -- The Portfolio on which the assets will be computed
    sync_date {datetime.date} -- The date on which the assets will be computed

Keyword Arguments:
    portfolio {portfolio.Portfolio} -- The core portfolio from which the computed position are created (default: {None})
    adjusted_weighting {int} -- the adjusted weight of the current level of index (default: {1})
    adjusted_currency_fx_rate {int} -- the adjusted currency exchange rate on the current level of index (default: {1})

Yields:
    tuple[dict, dict] -- Two dictionaries: One with filter parameters and one with default values

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.price_computation.PriceComputation.compute
documentation:
Given positions at t and t-1, we compute the performance and estimate the instrument price at t
If a price already exists at that date and is estimated already, we update it. If no price exists, we create it as estimated.
Otherwise, we don't do anything to protect imported real prices.
\:param instrument: The instrument to compute the new price from
\:param sync_date: The date at which we need to compute the new price
\:param task_execution_datetime: An optional datetime specifying at which time this task was initially executed.
\:param override_execution_datetime_validity: If true, we don't valide `task_execution_datetime`
\:param kwargs: keyword arguments

documentation: Utility function that returns the signature of the compute method

Gather all tasks that needs to run under this synchronization job as a list of celery signatures.

\:return: list[signature]

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.price_computation.PriceComputation._default_callback
documentation:
Default NAV computation function. We simply compute the performance given two positions for two dates and estimate the new price
based on the overall performance.
TODO: If exit/buy of positions, is this function still correct?
\:param instrument: The instrument to compute the new price from
\:param val_date: The date at which we need to compute the new price
\:param kwargs: keyword arguments

If a custom task is specified, return the loaded module as callabck. Otherwise, returns the default synchronization
function defined in \_default_callback
\:return: Callable

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask.schedule_str
documentation:
Returns the crontab string representation. If fitler_daily is true, we cast the crontab so that the lowest frequency
becomes daily.
\:param filter_daily: bool (optional)
\:return: crontab string representation

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask.dates_range
documentation:

\:param to_date: date

\:return: list[date]

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask._tasks_signature
documentation:
Gather all tasks that needs to run under this synchronization job as a list of celery signatures.
This method is expected to be implemented at each inheriting class.
\:param args: list
\:param kwargs: dict
\:return: list[signature]

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask._default_callback
documentation:
The default synchronization function executed if no custom task is provided for this synchronization object.
This method is expected to be implemented at each inheriting class.
\:param args: list
\:param kwargs: dict
\:return: callable

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask._get_kwargs
documentation:
return the base keyword argument to be injected into the `_tasks_signature` method. Define as a standalone function
in order to allow subclass definition.
\:return: dict

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask._end_task_signature
documentation:
A synchronization object can defined a dependant task that will be executed at this end of all returned task from
\_tasks_signatures.
This function returns the signature of this chained task.
\:param kwargs:
\:return: signature

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.synchronization.synchronization.SynchronizationTask.chord
documentation:
This function is the main entry point of the synchronization worklow. It is called from within the shared_task `task`
and create the celery chord containing the list of tasks chained to the end task (if any)
\:param kwargs:
\:return: chord

methods.name: wbportfolio.models.mixins.liquidity_stress_test.LiquidityStressMixin.get_product_ids_from_group_product_or_product
documentation:
The function returns a list of id(s) if:
    - a simple product id if instrument_type == Product.
    - the list of the product ids if instrument_type == ProductGroup.
    - the list of the products ids if instrument_type == Product and if the product belongs to a group.
Otherwise the function returns None.

methods.name: wbportfolio.pms.trading.handler.TradingService.is_valid
documentation:
Validate the trade batch against a set of default rules. Populate the validated_trades and errors property.
Ignore error by default
Args:

Returns: True if the trades batch is valid

methods.name: wbportfolio.pms.trading.handler.TradingService.normalize
documentation: Normalize the instantiate trades batch so that the target weight is 100%

This class represents the trading service. It can be instantiated either with the target portfolio and the effective portfolio or given a direct list of trade
In any case, it will compute all three states

inner_classes.name: wbportfolio.serializers.transactions.transactions.TransactionModelSerializer.Meta
documentation: def get_transaction_underlying_type(self, obj):
try:
casted_transaction = obj.get_casted_transaction()
return casted_transaction.Type[casted_transaction.transaction_subtype].label
except Exception as e:
return ""

methods.name: wbportfolio.tests.models.test_customer_trades.TestCustomerTradeModel.test_trade_delete_shift_claims
documentation:
Test main functionality: when a trade is linked to an approved claim and is marked for deletion, we try to find the closest trade non marked for deletion or pending and unclaimed.

If this succeed, the claim is forwarded to this other trade. Otherwise, it will fail.

functions.name: wbportfolio.defaults.portfolio.default_rebalancing.callback
documentation:
Recursively calculates the position for a portfolio

Arguments:
    portfolio {portfolio.Portfolio} -- The Portfolio on which the assets will be computed
    sync_date {datetime.date} -- The date on which the assets will be computed

Keyword Arguments:
    portfolio {portfolio.Portfolio} -- The core portfolio from which the computed position are created (default: {None})
    adjusted_weighting {int} -- the adjusted weight of the current level of index (default: {1})
    adjusted_currency_fx_rate {int} -- the adjusted currency exchange rate on the current level of index (default: {1})

Yields:
    tuple[dict, dict] -- Two dictionaries: One with filter parameters and one with default values

modules.name: wbportfolio.import_export.parsers.sg_lux.sylk
documentation:
Read SYLK files

Caveat emptor!  This module has only been tested with SYLK files generated
by AppleWorks 5.0!  It almost certainly needs work to be able to process
files generated by other spreadsheets.
Author: Skip Montanaro (skip@mojam.com)
Version: 0.1
https://github.com/smontanaro/python-bits/blob/master/sylk.py

methods.name: wbportfolio.viewsets.configs.endpoints.trades.TradeEndpointConfig.get_create_endpoint
documentation: def get_instance_endpoint(self, **kwargs):
try:
if self.request.user.has_perm("wbportfolio.administrate_trade"):
obj = self.view.get_object()
return reverse("wbportfolio:trade-detail", args=[obj.id], request=self.request)
except AssertionError:
pass
return reverse("wbportfolio:trade-list", request=self.request)

functions.name: wbportfolio.tasks.daily_instrument_price_statistics_synchronization
documentation: Daily synchronization tasks.
This tasks needs to be ran at maximum once a day in order to guarantee data consitency in
case of change in change (e.g. reimport).

functions.name: wbportfolio.tasks.update_preferred_classification_per_instrument_and_portfolio_as_task
documentation: A Task to run every day to update automatically the preferred classification
per instrument of each wbportfolio containing assets.

functions.name: wbportfolio.tasks.general_portfolio_synchronization_and_update
documentation: This task needs to run at fix interval. It will trigger the basic wbportfolio synchronization update:
- Fetch for stainly price at t-1
- propagate (or update) t-2 asset positions into t-1
- Synchronize wbportfolio at t-1
- Compute Instrument Price estimate at t-1
